content.question: What is the causal contribution of the 6-/8-year gymnázium track structure *per se* to Hungary's exceptionally high socioeconomic-status/performance gradient in PISA, versus other factors such as residential segregation, within-school sorting, private-school enrollment, teacher quality, or family educational investment?
content.reduction_path: Use structural equation modeling or decomposition analysis to partition the total PISA gradient into components attributable to (a) primary-school peer effects and segregation, (b) selection into and effects of secondary-track type, (c) private vs. public school enrollment, (d) within-school tracking, and (e) unobserved family factors; compare models estimated before and after hypothetically removing formal track selection from the education system.
